{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|陸奥国 (1869-)}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|陸奥国 (1869-)}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

{{Redirect|Tsugaru|Other uses|Tsugaru (disambiguation)}} {{Pathnav|[[Japan]]|[[Tōhoku region]]|[[Northern Tōhoku]]|[[Aomori Prefecture]]|frame=1}} {| class="wikitable" style="float:right; margin:1em 0em 1em 1em; width:16em;" |colspan="2" style="text-align:center; background-color:#6ede6e;"|'''Data on the Tsugaru Region''' |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|Area |style="text-align:right;"|'''4828.08''' [[square kilometers|km2]]
(50.06% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO/201010/opening.htm October 1, 2010, National Area Statistics] (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Census of Japan|Census]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''774,617''' people
(56.4% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|陸奥国 (1869-)}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)

(56.4% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|陸奥国 (1869-)}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|陸奥国 (1869-)}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|陸奥国 (1869-)}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|陸奥国 (1869-)}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|陸奥国 (1869-)}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[Category:New]]

The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]]

{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]]

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)

{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|陸奥国 (1869-)}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tsugaru Province-qq]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]]

(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|陸奥国 (1869-)}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}}. In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象}} in the southern areas. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsugaru Province]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]]
