({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|12=draft}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|12=draft}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|12=draft}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|12=draft}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|12=draft}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|12=draft}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市|12=draft}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市|12=draft}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市|12=draft}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川|12=draft}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島|12=draft}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町|12=draft}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|12=draft}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾|12=draft}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|12=draft}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|12=draft}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|12=draft}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|12=draft}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象|12=draft}} in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|12=draft}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|12=draft}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:S11]] [[Category:s11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|12=draft}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|12=draft}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|12=draft}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|12=draft}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|12=draft}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|12=draft}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|12=draft}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市|12=draft}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市|12=draft}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市|12=draft}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川|12=draft}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島|12=draft}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町|12=draft}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|12=draft}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾|12=draft}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|12=draft}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|12=draft}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|12=draft}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|12=draft}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象|12=draft}} in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|12=draft}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|12=draft}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)

The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川|12=draft|12=draft}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島|12=draft|12=draft}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町|12=draft|12=draft}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|12=draft|12=draft}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾|12=draft|12=draft}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|12=draft|12=draft}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|12=draft|12=draft}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|12=draft|12=draft}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|12=draft|12=draft}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象|12=draft|12=draft}} in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}

{{Redirect|Tsugaru|Other uses|Tsugaru (disambiguation)}} {{Pathnav|[[Japan]]|[[Tōhoku region]]|[[Northern Tōhoku]]|[[Aomori Prefecture]]|frame=1}} {| class="wikitable" style="float:right; margin:1em 0em 1em 1em; width:16em;" |colspan="2" style="text-align:center; background-color:#6ede6e;"|'''Data on the Tsugaru Region''' |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|Area |style="text-align:right;"|'''4828.08''' [[square kilometers|km2]]
(50.06% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO/201010/opening.htm October 1, 2010, National Area Statistics] (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Census of Japan|Census]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''774,617''' people
(56.4% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}}

{{Redirect|津軽|その他の津軽|津軽 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{Pathnav|[[日本]]|[[東北地方]]|[[北東北]]|[[青森県]]|frame=1}} {| class="wikitable" style="float:right; margin:1em 0em 1em 1em; width:16em;" |colspan="2" style="text-align:center; background-color:#6ede6e;"|'''津軽地方'''のデータ |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|面積 |style="text-align:right;"|'''4828.08''' [[平方キロメートル|km2]]
（全県比：50.06%）
（2010年10月1日）[http://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO/201010/opening.htm 平成22年全国都道府県市区町村別面積調]（[[国土交通省]][[国土地理院]]） |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[国勢調査 (日本)|国勢調査]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''774,617''' 人
（全県比：56.4%）
（2010年10月1日）[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html 青森県統計データランド]（青森県） |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[推計人口]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' 人
（全県比：{{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}%）
（{{自治体人口/青森県|date}}） |} [[ファイル:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}緑 ： 津軽地方
{{Color|white|■}}白 ： [[南部地方 (青森県)|南部地方]]]] '''津軽地方'''（つがるちほう）とは、現在の[[青森県]]西部を指して言う地域呼称。[[江戸時代]]に[[津軽氏]]が支配した領域（[[弘前藩]]・[[黒石藩]]の領域）および[[津軽郡 (陸奥国)|津軽郡]]の領域にほぼ相当する。 [[明治維新]]で[[陸奥国]]（むつのくに）が分割されて設置された[[陸奥国 (1869-)|陸奥国]]（りくおうのくに。青森県全域と[[岩手県]]西北部）の異称「津軽国」とは異なる。 ==定義・区分== [[津軽郡 (陸奥国)|津軽郡]]や[[津軽氏]]領の[[弘前藩]]・[[黒石藩]]の範囲については当該記事を参照。 広義の津軽地方は、[[弘前市]]を中心にした南部の「[[弘前都市圏|中弘南黒（ちゅうこうなんこく）地区]]」、[[五所川原市]]を中心とした北西部の「[[西北五地方|西北五（せいほくご）地区]]」、[[青森市]]を中心とした北東部の「[[東青地域|東青（とうせい）地区]]」の3つの地区で構成される。津軽と呼ばれる地域が津軽平野南部から拡大してきた歴史的な経緯から、中心を中弘南黒地区と見て、中弘南黒地区のみ、あるいは、中弘南黒地区と西北五地区の2つの地区で狭義の津軽地方とする場合もある。狭義を用いる場合は、東青地区は「青森地方」などと呼ぶ。また、[[岩木川]]がつくる[[津軽平野]]を中心とした地域圏と認識される場合もある。なお、西北五地区から[[津軽半島]]にかけての地域を'''奥津軽'''と呼ぶこともある。 [[夏泊半島]]（[[平内町]]地域）は[[鎌倉時代]]の初めまでは[[糠部郡]]の一部であり、[[陸奥湾]]岸（青森市と東津軽郡の地域）は[[外ヶ浜]]といわれ、中世までは津軽に含めないこともあったが、近世以降は津軽郡が設置されてその一部となった。 また、西南端域（旧[[岩崎村 (青森県)|岩崎村]]地域）は中世までは[[出羽国]][[檜山郡 (出羽国)|檜山郡]]に含まれていたと推定されている[[村井章介]]・[[斉藤利男]]・[[小口雅史]]編 『北の環日本海世界』 山川出版社、2002年、ISBN 4634605309が、[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に津軽氏と[[佐竹氏]]の交渉により津軽領となった[http://www.town.fukaura.aomori.jp/fukaura.newHP/b_kankou/rekisi.bunka/rekisi/rekisi.files/rekisi.htm 「深浦町の歴史」（深浦町公式サイト内）]。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! colspan="4" | 地域呼称 !! colspan="2" | 郡 !! colspan="2" | 市 !! 青森県地域区分図 |- ! rowspan="5" | 津軽地方 | 青森地方 | colspan="2" | 東青 | style="width:1em;background-color:#c8c56e" | || [[東津軽郡|'''東'''津軽郡]] | style="width:1em;background-color:#bdba46" | || [[青森市|'''青'''森市]] | rowspan="5" | [[ファイル:青森県地域区分図.svg|200px]] |- | rowspan="4" | 津軽地方
（狭義） | rowspan="2" | 中弘南黒
（中南） | 中弘 | style="width:1em;background-color:#e68e8c" | || [[中津軽郡|'''中'''津軽郡]] | style="width:1em;background-color:#e6716e" | || [[弘前市|'''弘'''前市]] |- | 南黒 | style="width:1em;background-color:#e68e8c" | || [[南津軽郡|'''南'''津軽郡]] | style="width:1em;background-color:#e6716e" | || [[黒石市|'''黒'''石市]]
[[平川市]] |- | rowspan="2" | 西北五
（西北） | style="background-color:#ccc;"|  | style="width:1em;background-color:#8b8fe6" | || [[西津軽郡|'''西'''津軽郡]] | style="width:1em;background-color:#6d72e6" | || [[つがる市]] |- | 北五 | style="width:1em;background-color:#8b8fe6" | || [[北津軽郡|'''北'''津軽郡]] | style="width:1em;background-color:#6d72e6" | || [[五所川原市|'''五'''所川原市]] |} ===青森県庁=== [[2006年]]（平成18年）4月に[[青森県庁]]は県内を6つの地区に区分[http://portal.pref.aomori.lg.jp/townmap.html 青森県庁による地域区分]{{リンク切れ|date=2010年1月}}し、各地域に総合的な[[出先機関]]である[[支庁|地域県民局]]を設置したが[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/kenmin/ 地域県民局]（青森県）[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/kenmin/hi-renkei/gaiyo.html 東青地域県民局の概要]（青森県）[http://reiki.pref.aomori.lg.jp/reiki_honbun/ac00100101.html 青森県地域県民局及び行政機関設置条例]（青森県）、これら地域県民局は[[2025年]]（令和7年）[[3月31日]]で廃止された{{Cite web|url= https://www.toonippo.co.jp/articles/-/1990266|title= 地域県民局廃止／知事への権限集中に懸念も|publisher=東奥日報|access-date=2025-04-02}}。 以下の3つの地区が広義の津軽地方に相当した。 {| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:right;" |+青森県庁による区分 !class="unsortable"|名称 !class="unsortable"|所管区域 !面積
(km2) !人口
（人） |- !東青地域県民局 |style="text-align:left; background-color:#c8c56e;"|'''青森市'''、東津軽郡 |1477.37 |{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} round 0}}}} |- !中南地域県民局 |style="text-align:left; background-color:#e68e8c;"|'''弘前市'''、黒石市、平川市、中津軽郡、南津軽郡 |1555.92 |{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} round 0}}}} |- ! 西北地域県民局 |style="text-align:left; background-color:#8b8fe6;"|'''五所川原市'''、つがる市、西津軽郡、北津軽郡 |1794.79 |{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}} |} * 地域県民局の所在都市は太字。 * 面積は2010年（平成22年）10月1日現在 * 人口は{{自治体人口/青森県|date}}現在の[[推計人口]]。 ===気象庁=== [[気象庁]]の地域区分[https://www.jma-net.go.jp/aomori/koho/sonota/faq.html#saibun 青森県の警報・注意報の発表地域区分]（[[気象庁]]青森[[地方気象台]]） では、広義の津軽地方が一次細分区域の「津軽」と定義される。二次細分区域では、西北五地区が「北五津軽」と「西津軽」に2分割される。東青地区は「東青津軽」、中弘南黒地区は「中南津軽」に相当する。 {|class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" !一次細分区域 !二次細分区域 !該当自治体 |- |rowspan="4"|津軽||東青津軽||青森市、東津軽郡（[[平内町]]、[[今別町]]、[[蓬田村]]、[[外ヶ浜町]]） |- |北五津軽||五所川原市、北津軽郡（[[板柳町]]、[[鶴田町]]、[[中泊町]]） |- |西津軽||つがる市、西津軽郡（[[鰺ヶ沢町]]、[[深浦町]]） |- |中南津軽||弘前市、黒石市、平川市
中津軽郡（[[西目屋村]]）、南津軽郡（[[藤崎町]]、[[大鰐町]]、[[田舎館村]]） |} ==拠点の変遷== 中世には岩木川河口の[[十三湊]]があった「西北五地区」が貿易（国際・国内）で活況を呈したが、[[南部氏]]の侵入により衰退した。 江戸時代には[[弘前城]]が置かれ[[城下町]]が開かれた[[弘前市|弘前]]のある「[[弘前都市圏#地域圏|中弘南黒地区]]」が中心になった。日本海側の[[鰺ヶ沢町|鰺ヶ沢]]・[[深浦町|深浦]]は[[北前船]]の寄港地となって栄えた。特に、鰺ヶ沢は津軽産米の積出港として、最も重要視された。 明治時代になり、[[廃藩置県]]により旧藩を引き継いだ弘前県（弘前藩）、黒石県（黒石藩）、斗南県（[[斗南藩]]）、七戸県（[[七戸藩]]）、八戸県（[[八戸藩]]）と北海道渡島半島の館県（[[館藩]]）の6県が合併し、弘前県が成立した。この際に県庁は弘前に置かれたが、初代県大参事の野田豁通が県庁を現在の青森市に移転し、県名を青森県とすることを決定した。さらに本州の鉄道の北のターミナル、および北海道との窓口となって[[青函連絡船]]が就航するようになったため、青森が存在感を増すことになった。 その後、弘前市は[[第8師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第八師団]]と[[弘前高等学校 (旧制)|旧制弘前高校]]を擁した軍事・学園都市として、1889年（明治22年）の統計では、弘前市は人口30,487人で全国29位、[[東北地方]]では[[仙台市]]、[[盛岡市]]に次いで3位だった。 戦後、日本軍の解体により第八師団も解散したが、[[弘前大学]]が新設されたことから、弘前市は引き続き[[学園都市#類似概念|学園都市]]としての性格を保ち続けている。 [[白神山地]]の[[世界遺産]]登録を期に、[[弘前ねぷた]]・[[平川ねぷた]]・[[黒石ねぷた]]・[[五所川原立佞武多]]をはじめとする[[ねぷた]]などの文化や雄大な自然を有する津軽地方は、観光地として集客能力を増している。 ==気候== [[日本海側気候]]であり、冬に[[雪]]が多いのが最大の特徴である。山間部の積雪量が多い。 夏は最高気温が30度程度まで上がる日もあるが、それほど暑さは長続きしない。[[盆地]]の形状に似た南部の「中弘南黒地区」は[[フェーン現象]]が発生して気温が高い一方、「西北五地区」「東青地区」では、しばしば[[やませ]]の影響を受け、気温が上がらず、農作物に影響が出る年もある。ただし、やませが直接吹き付ける[[南部地方 (青森県)|南部地方]]（青森県の東半分）に比べれば気温は高い。 天気予報の地域区分については[[#気象庁]]を参照。 ==歴史== 歴史文献では[[655年]]『[[日本書紀]]』の[[斉明天皇]]元年から登場し、畿内から最も遠い[[蝦夷]]の居住地が「津苅」「東日流」「津刈」「都加留」などと表記されている。中世には「[[平賀郡]]（津軽平賀郡）」「[[鼻和郡]]（津軽鼻和郡）」「[[田舎郡]]（津軽田舎郡）」の3つの郡（[[山辺郡_(陸奥国)|山辺郡]]（津軽山辺郡）」が分立していた時期は4つの郡）に分けて把握され、「津軽三郡」（または「津軽四郡」）と言われた。 鎌倉時代は[[得宗|北条得宗]]領として[[安東氏]]などが支配したが、14世紀になると、[[南部氏]]が支配するようになる。戦国時代に[[津軽氏]]が支配した。近世になると、それまでの「平賀郡」「鼻和郡」「田舎郡」の3郡がまとめられて「津軽郡」となる。[[明治維新]]のあと、青森県の一部となる。[[1878年]]（明治11年）、東津軽郡・西津軽郡・南津軽郡・北津軽郡・中津軽郡に分けられた。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[南部地方 (青森県)]] * [[下北地方]] * [[西北五地方]] * [[津軽郡 (陸奥国)]] * [[青森県]] * [[雁風呂]] * [[弘前都市圏]] * [[五所川原都市圏]] * [[青森都市圏]] * [[津軽弁]] * [[津軽三味線]] * [[津軽と南部]] * [[津軽選挙]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ 津軽広域連合]（中弘南黒地区の市町村連合） {{Japan-geo-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つかるちほう}} [[Category:青森県の地域]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[津軽地方]] to [[Tsugaru Province]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Redirect|津軽|その他の津軽|津軽 (曖昧さ回避)}} {{Pathnav|[[日本]]|[[東北地方]]|[[北東北]]|[[青森県]]|frame=1}} {| class="wikitable" style="float:right; margin:1em 0em 1em 1em; width:16em;" |colspan="2" style="text-align:center; background-color:#6ede6e;"|'''津軽地方'''のデータ |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|面積 |style="text-align:right;"|'''4828.08''' [[平方キロメートル|km2]]
（全県比：50.06%）
（2010年10月1日）[http://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO/201010/opening.htm 平成22年全国都道府県市区町村別面積調]（[[国土交通省]][[国土地理院]]） |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[国勢調査 (日本)|国勢調査]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''774,617''' 人
（全県比：56.4%）
（2010年10月1日）[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html 青森県統計データランド]（青森県） |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[推計人口]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' 人
（全県比：{{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}%）
（{{自治体人口/青森県|date}}） |} [[ファイル:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}緑 ： 津軽地方
{{Color|white|■}}白 ： [[南部地方 (青森県)|南部地方]]]] '''津軽地方'''（つがるちほう）とは、現在の[[青森県]]西部を指して言う地域呼称。[[江戸時代]]に[[津軽氏]]が支配した領域（[[弘前藩]]・[[黒石藩]]の領域）および[[津軽郡 (陸奥国)|津軽郡]]の領域にほぼ相当する。 [[明治維新]]で[[陸奥国]]（むつのくに）が分割されて設置された[[陸奥国 (1869-)|陸奥国]]（りくおうのくに。青森県全域と[[岩手県]]西北部）の異称「津軽国」とは異なる。 ==定義・区分== [[津軽郡 (陸奥国)|津軽郡]]や[[津軽氏]]領の[[弘前藩]]・[[黒石藩]]の範囲については当該記事を参照。 広義の津軽地方は、[[弘前市]]を中心にした南部の「[[弘前都市圏|中弘南黒（ちゅうこうなんこく）地区]]」、[[五所川原市]]を中心とした北西部の「[[西北五地方|西北五（せいほくご）地区]]」、[[青森市]]を中心とした北東部の「[[東青地域|東青（とうせい）地区]]」の3つの地区で構成される。津軽と呼ばれる地域が津軽平野南部から拡大してきた歴史的な経緯から、中心を中弘南黒地区と見て、中弘南黒地区のみ、あるいは、中弘南黒地区と西北五地区の2つの地区で狭義の津軽地方とする場合もある。狭義を用いる場合は、東青地区は「青森地方」などと呼ぶ。また、[[岩木川]]がつくる[[津軽平野]]を中心とした地域圏と認識される場合もある。なお、西北五地区から[[津軽半島]]にかけての地域を'''奥津軽'''と呼ぶこともある。 [[夏泊半島]]（[[平内町]]地域）は[[鎌倉時代]]の初めまでは[[糠部郡]]の一部であり、[[陸奥湾]]岸（青森市と東津軽郡の地域）は[[外ヶ浜]]といわれ、中世までは津軽に含めないこともあったが、近世以降は津軽郡が設置されてその一部となった。 また、西南端域（旧[[岩崎村 (青森県)|岩崎村]]地域）は中世までは[[出羽国]][[檜山郡 (出羽国)|檜山郡]]に含まれていたと推定されている[[村井章介]]・[[斉藤利男]]・[[小口雅史]]編 『北の環日本海世界』 山川出版社、2002年、ISBN 4634605309が、[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）に津軽氏と[[佐竹氏]]の交渉により津軽領となった[http://www.town.fukaura.aomori.jp/fukaura.newHP/b_kankou/rekisi.bunka/rekisi/rekisi.files/rekisi.htm 「深浦町の歴史」（深浦町公式サイト内）]。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" ! colspan="4" | 地域呼称 !! colspan="2" | 郡 !! colspan="2" | 市 !! 青森県地域区分図 |- ! rowspan="5" | 津軽地方 | 青森地方 | colspan="2" | 東青 | style="width:1em;background-color:#c8c56e" | || [[東津軽郡|'''東'''津軽郡]] | style="width:1em;background-color:#bdba46" | || [[青森市|'''青'''森市]] | rowspan="5" | [[ファイル:青森県地域区分図.svg|200px]] |- | rowspan="4" | 津軽地方
（狭義） | rowspan="2" | 中弘南黒
（中南） | 中弘 | style="width:1em;background-color:#e68e8c" | || [[中津軽郡|'''中'''津軽郡]] | style="width:1em;background-color:#e6716e" | || [[弘前市|'''弘'''前市]] |- | 南黒 | style="width:1em;background-color:#e68e8c" | || [[南津軽郡|'''南'''津軽郡]] | style="width:1em;background-color:#e6716e" | || [[黒石市|'''黒'''石市]]
[[平川市]] |- | rowspan="2" | 西北五
（西北） | style="background-color:#ccc;"|  | style="width:1em;background-color:#8b8fe6" | || [[西津軽郡|'''西'''津軽郡]] | style="width:1em;background-color:#6d72e6" | || [[つがる市]] |- | 北五 | style="width:1em;background-color:#8b8fe6" | || [[北津軽郡|'''北'''津軽郡]] | style="width:1em;background-color:#6d72e6" | || [[五所川原市|'''五'''所川原市]] |} ===青森県庁=== [[2006年]]（平成18年）4月に[[青森県庁]]は県内を6つの地区に区分[http://portal.pref.aomori.lg.jp/townmap.html 青森県庁による地域区分]{{リンク切れ|date=2010年1月}}し、各地域に総合的な[[出先機関]]である[[支庁|地域県民局]]を設置したが[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/kenmin/ 地域県民局]（青森県）[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/soshiki/kenmin/hi-renkei/gaiyo.html 東青地域県民局の概要]（青森県）[http://reiki.pref.aomori.lg.jp/reiki_honbun/ac00100101.html 青森県地域県民局及び行政機関設置条例]（青森県）、これら地域県民局は[[2025年]]（令和7年）[[3月31日]]で廃止された{{Cite web|url= https://www.toonippo.co.jp/articles/-/1990266|title= 地域県民局廃止／知事への権限集中に懸念も|publisher=東奥日報|access-date=2025-04-02}}。 以下の3つの地区が広義の津軽地方に相当した。 {| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:right;" |+青森県庁による区分 !class="unsortable"|名称 !class="unsortable"|所管区域 !面積
(km2) !人口
（人） |- !東青地域県民局 |style="text-align:left; background-color:#c8c56e;"|'''青森市'''、東津軽郡 |1477.37 |{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} round 0}}}} |- !中南地域県民局 |style="text-align:left; background-color:#e68e8c;"|'''弘前市'''、黒石市、平川市、中津軽郡、南津軽郡 |1555.92 |{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} round 0}}}} |- ! 西北地域県民局 |style="text-align:left; background-color:#8b8fe6;"|'''五所川原市'''、つがる市、西津軽郡、北津軽郡 |1794.79 |{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}} |} * 地域県民局の所在都市は太字。 * 面積は2010年（平成22年）10月1日現在 * 人口は{{自治体人口/青森県|date}}現在の[[推計人口]]。 ===気象庁=== [[気象庁]]の地域区分[https://www.jma-net.go.jp/aomori/koho/sonota/faq.html#saibun 青森県の警報・注意報の発表地域区分]（[[気象庁]]青森[[地方気象台]]） では、広義の津軽地方が一次細分区域の「津軽」と定義される。二次細分区域では、西北五地区が「北五津軽」と「西津軽」に2分割される。東青地区は「東青津軽」、中弘南黒地区は「中南津軽」に相当する。 {|class="wikitable" style="text-align:center" !一次細分区域 !二次細分区域 !該当自治体 |- |rowspan="4"|津軽||東青津軽||青森市、東津軽郡（[[平内町]]、[[今別町]]、[[蓬田村]]、[[外ヶ浜町]]） |- |北五津軽||五所川原市、北津軽郡（[[板柳町]]、[[鶴田町]]、[[中泊町]]） |- |西津軽||つがる市、西津軽郡（[[鰺ヶ沢町]]、[[深浦町]]） |- |中南津軽||弘前市、黒石市、平川市
中津軽郡（[[西目屋村]]）、南津軽郡（[[藤崎町]]、[[大鰐町]]、[[田舎館村]]） |} ==拠点の変遷== 中世には岩木川河口の[[十三湊]]があった「西北五地区」が貿易（国際・国内）で活況を呈したが、[[南部氏]]の侵入により衰退した。 江戸時代には[[弘前城]]が置かれ[[城下町]]が開かれた[[弘前市|弘前]]のある「[[弘前都市圏#地域圏|中弘南黒地区]]」が中心になった。日本海側の[[鰺ヶ沢町|鰺ヶ沢]]・[[深浦町|深浦]]は[[北前船]]の寄港地となって栄えた。特に、鰺ヶ沢は津軽産米の積出港として、最も重要視された。 明治時代になり、[[廃藩置県]]により旧藩を引き継いだ弘前県（弘前藩）、黒石県（黒石藩）、斗南県（[[斗南藩]]）、七戸県（[[七戸藩]]）、八戸県（[[八戸藩]]）と北海道渡島半島の館県（[[館藩]]）の6県が合併し、弘前県が成立した。この際に県庁は弘前に置かれたが、初代県大参事の野田豁通が県庁を現在の青森市に移転し、県名を青森県とすることを決定した。さらに本州の鉄道の北のターミナル、および北海道との窓口となって[[青函連絡船]]が就航するようになったため、青森が存在感を増すことになった。 その後、弘前市は[[第8師団 (日本軍)|陸軍第八師団]]と[[弘前高等学校 (旧制)|旧制弘前高校]]を擁した軍事・学園都市として、1889年（明治22年）の統計では、弘前市は人口30,487人で全国29位、[[東北地方]]では[[仙台市]]、[[盛岡市]]に次いで3位だった。 戦後、日本軍の解体により第八師団も解散したが、[[弘前大学]]が新設されたことから、弘前市は引き続き[[学園都市#類似概念|学園都市]]としての性格を保ち続けている。 [[白神山地]]の[[世界遺産]]登録を期に、[[弘前ねぷた]]・[[平川ねぷた]]・[[黒石ねぷた]]・[[五所川原立佞武多]]をはじめとする[[ねぷた]]などの文化や雄大な自然を有する津軽地方は、観光地として集客能力を増している。 ==気候== [[日本海側気候]]であり、冬に[[雪]]が多いのが最大の特徴である。山間部の積雪量が多い。 夏は最高気温が30度程度まで上がる日もあるが、それほど暑さは長続きしない。[[盆地]]の形状に似た南部の「中弘南黒地区」は[[フェーン現象]]が発生して気温が高い一方、「西北五地区」「東青地区」では、しばしば[[やませ]]の影響を受け、気温が上がらず、農作物に影響が出る年もある。ただし、やませが直接吹き付ける[[南部地方 (青森県)|南部地方]]（青森県の東半分）に比べれば気温は高い。 天気予報の地域区分については[[#気象庁]]を参照。 ==歴史== 歴史文献では[[655年]]『[[日本書紀]]』の[[斉明天皇]]元年から登場し、畿内から最も遠い[[蝦夷]]の居住地が「津苅」「東日流」「津刈」「都加留」などと表記されている。中世には「[[平賀郡]]（津軽平賀郡）」「[[鼻和郡]]（津軽鼻和郡）」「[[田舎郡]]（津軽田舎郡）」の3つの郡（[[山辺郡_(陸奥国)|山辺郡]]（津軽山辺郡）」が分立していた時期は4つの郡）に分けて把握され、「津軽三郡」（または「津軽四郡」）と言われた。 鎌倉時代は[[得宗|北条得宗]]領として[[安東氏]]などが支配したが、14世紀になると、[[南部氏]]が支配するようになる。戦国時代に[[津軽氏]]が支配した。近世になると、それまでの「平賀郡」「鼻和郡」「田舎郡」の3郡がまとめられて「津軽郡」となる。[[明治維新]]のあと、青森県の一部となる。[[1878年]]（明治11年）、東津軽郡・西津軽郡・南津軽郡・北津軽郡・中津軽郡に分けられた。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[南部地方 (青森県)]] * [[下北地方]] * [[西北五地方]] * [[津軽郡 (陸奥国)]] * [[青森県]] * [[雁風呂]] * [[弘前都市圏]] * [[五所川原都市圏]] * [[青森都市圏]] * [[津軽弁]] * [[津軽三味線]] * [[津軽と南部]] * [[津軽選挙]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ 津軽広域連合]（中弘南黒地区の市町村連合） {{Japan-geo-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つかるちほう}} [[Category:青森県の地域]]

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}} {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}}

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)

{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsugaru Province}} {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

{{Redirect|Tsugaru|Other uses|Tsugaru (disambiguation)}} {{Pathnav|[[Japan]]|[[Tōhoku region]]|[[Northern Tōhoku]]|[[Aomori Prefecture]]|frame=1}} {| class="wikitable" style="float:right; margin:1em 0em 1em 1em; width:16em;" |colspan="2" style="text-align:center; background-color:#6ede6e;"|'''Data on the Tsugaru Region''' |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|Area |style="text-align:right;"|'''4828.08''' [[square kilometers|km2]]
(50.06% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO/201010/opening.htm October 1, 2010, National Area Statistics] (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Census of Japan|Census]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''774,617''' people
(56.4% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)

(56.4% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Prefecture|qq=}}, corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru clan|qq=}} during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, which included the domains of {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Domain|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}}. This region differs from the post-{{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}} division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|ja|岩手県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwate Prefecture|qq=}}. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} and the domains controlled by the {{ill|Hirosaki Domain|ja|弘前藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Domain|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on {{ill|Hirosaki City|ja|弘前市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki City|qq=}} in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on {{ill|Goshogawara City|ja|五所川原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goshogawara City|qq=}} in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on {{ill|Aomori City|ja|青森市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori City|qq=}} in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the {{ill|Iwaki River|ja|岩木川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki River|qq=}} and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the {{ill|Tsugaru Peninsula|ja|津軽半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru Peninsula|qq=}} is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The {{ill|Natsudomari Peninsula|ja|夏泊半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsudomari Peninsula|qq=}} ({{ill|Hiranai Town|ja|平内町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiranai Town|qq=}} area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}. - The coastal area around {{ill|Mutsu Bay|ja|陸奥湾|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Bay|qq=}} was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the {{ill|Emishi|ja|蝦夷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emishi|qq=}}. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the {{ill|Nanbu clan|ja|南部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu clan|qq=}} in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the {{ill|Tsugaru clan|ja|津軽氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru clan|qq=}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the {{ill|Föhn phenomenon|ja|フェーン現象|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Föhn phenomenon|qq=}} in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Prefecture|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru dialect|ja|津軽弁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru dialect|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru shamisen|ja|津軽三味線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru shamisen|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Tsugaru Province]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

{{Redirect|Tsugaru|Other uses|Tsugaru (disambiguation)}} {{Pathnav|[[Japan]]|[[Tōhoku region]]|[[Northern Tōhoku]]|[[Aomori Prefecture]]|frame=1}} {| class="wikitable" style="float:right; margin:1em 0em 1em 1em; width:16em;" |colspan="2" style="text-align:center; background-color:#6ede6e;"|'''Data on the Tsugaru Region''' |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|Area |style="text-align:right;"|'''4828.08''' [[square kilometers|km2]]
(50.06% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO/201010/opening.htm October 1, 2010, National Area Statistics] (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Census of Japan|Census]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''774,617''' people
(56.4% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}}. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru Province" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru Province]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]
