({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region

{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=}}, as well as the area historically known as {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}}. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru region" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (Mutsu Province )|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡 (陸奥国)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Moved pages]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

(50.06% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO/201010/opening.htm October 1, 2010, National Area Statistics] (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Census of Japan|Census]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''774,617''' people
(56.4% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=}}, as well as the area historically known as [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]]. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru region" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]] * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Moved pages]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]]

(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, as well as the area historically known as [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]]. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru region" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外が浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]] * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] [[Category:Moved pages]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[ja:津軽地方]] [[zh:津輕地方]]

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, as well as the area historically known as [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]]. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru region" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外が浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]] * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Regions of Aomori Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[ja:津軽地方]] [[zh:津輕地方]]

({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)

In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外が浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[:en:Meteorological Agency|Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]] * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[ja:津軽地方]] [[zh:津輕地方]]

{{Redirect|Tsugaru|Other uses|Tsugaru (disambiguation)}} {{Pathnav|[[Japan]]|[[Tōhoku region]]|[[Northern Tōhoku]]|[[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]|frame=1}} {| class="wikitable" style="float:right; margin:1em 0em 1em 1em; width:16em;" |colspan="2" style="text-align:center; background-color:#6ede6e;"|'''Data on the Tsugaru Region''' |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|Area |style="text-align:right;"|'''4828.08''' [[:en:square kilometers|km2]]
(50.06% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO/201010/opening.htm October 1, 2010, National Area Statistics] (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Census of Japan|Census]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''774,617''' people
(56.4% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, as well as the area historically known as [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]]. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru region" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外が浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[:en:Meteorological Agency|Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]] * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[ja:%E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[ja:津軽地方]] [[zh:津輕地方]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Redirect|Tsugaru|Other uses|Tsugaru (disambiguation)}} {{Pathnav|[[Japan]]|[[Tōhoku region]]|[[Northern Tōhoku]]|[[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]]|frame=1}} {| class="wikitable" style="float:right; margin:1em 0em 1em 1em; width:16em;" |colspan="2" style="text-align:center; background-color:#6ede6e;"|'''Data on the Tsugaru Region''' |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|Area |style="text-align:right;"|'''4828.08''' [[:en:square kilometers|km2]]
(50.06% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.gsi.go.jp/KOKUJYOHO/MENCHO/201010/opening.htm October 1, 2010, National Area Statistics] (Geospatial Information Authority of Japan, Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Census of Japan|Census]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''774,617''' people
(56.4% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, as well as the area historically known as [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]]. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru region" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外が浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[:en:Meteorological Agency|Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]] * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[sn:E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[ja:津軽地方]] [[zh:津輕地方]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

(56.4% of the prefecture)
(as of October 1, 2010)[http://www.pref.aomori.lg.jp/kensei/tokei/dataland.html Aomori Prefecture Data Land] (Aomori Prefecture) |- |style="text-align:center; background-color:#eee;"|[[Estimated population]] |style="text-align:right;"|'''{{formatnum:{{ #expr: {{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}} round 0}}}}''' people
({{formatnum:{{#expr:({{自治体人口/青森県|青森市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平内町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|今別町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|蓬田村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|外ヶ浜町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|弘前市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|黒石市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|平川市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|藤崎町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|大鰐町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|田舎館村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|西目屋村}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|五所川原市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|つがる市}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|板柳町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鶴田町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|中泊町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|鰺ヶ沢町}} + {{自治体人口/青森県|深浦町}}) *100 / {{自治体人口/青森県|青森県}} round 1}}}}% of the prefecture)
(as of {{自治体人口/青森県|date}}) |} [[File:Aomori Tsugaru-block.png|thumb|250px|{{Color|#6ede6e|■}}Green: Tsugaru region
{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, as well as the area historically known as [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]]. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru region" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外が浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[:en:Meteorological Agency|Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]] * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[sn:E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[ja:津軽地方]] [[zh:津輕地方]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, as well as the area historically known as [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]]. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru region" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外が浜}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[:en:Meteorological Agency|Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=}} * [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]] * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[sn:E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[ja:津軽地方]] [[zh:津輕地方]]

{{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩|1=Hirosaki Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q903555}}, as well as the area historically known as [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]]. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru region" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=|1=Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16071541}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩|1=Hirosaki Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q903555}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=|1=Nukanobu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604784}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外が浜|1=Sotogahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11252073}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[:en:Meteorological Agency|Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=|1=Andō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450838}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=|1=Japan Sea side climate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11086452}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=|1=Nanbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408519}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=|1=Shimokita region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360647}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=|1=Seihoku-Go Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627298}} * [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]] * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=|1=Tsugaru and Nanbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556385}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=|1=Tsugaru elections|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215206}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsugaru region]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[sn:E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[ja:津軽地方]] [[zh:津輕地方]]

The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." {{Color|white|■}}White: [[Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|Nanbu region]]]] '''Tsugaru Region''' (津軽地方) refers to the western part of present-day [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]], corresponding approximately to the territories controlled by the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], which included the domains of [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩|1=Hirosaki Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q903555}}, as well as the area historically known as [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]]. This region differs from the post-[[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]] division of {{ill|Mutsu Province (1869–)|ja|Mutsu Province (1869-)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsu Province (1869–)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} into several territories, where "Tsugaru region" was used as an alternative name for all of Aomori Prefecture and the northwest part of [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]. == Definitions and Subdivisions == For details on the {{ill|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|ja|津軽郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|qq=|1=Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16071541}} and the domains controlled by the [[:en:Hirosaki Domain|Hirosaki Domain]] and {{ill|Kuroishi Domain|ja|黒石藩|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroishi Domain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弘前藩|1=Hirosaki Domain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q903555}}, refer to the respective articles. The broader Tsugaru region consists of three major areas: 1. The "Chūkō Nankoku Area" (中弘南黒地区), centered on [[:en:Hirosaki|Hirosaki City]] in the south. 2. The "Seihoku-Go Area" (西北五地区), centered on [[:en:Goshogawara|Goshogawara City]] in the northwest. 3. The "Tōsei Area" (東青地区), centered on [[:en:Aomori|Aomori City]] in the northeast. Historically, the term "Tsugaru" initially referred to the southern part of the Tsugaru Plain but later expanded to encompass these regions. The narrower definition of Tsugaru often includes only the Chūkō Nankoku Area or both the Chūkō Nankoku and Seihoku-Go Areas, while excluding the Tōsei Area, which is sometimes called "Aomori Region." In some contexts, Tsugaru also refers specifically to areas along the [[:en:Iwaki River|Iwaki River]] and the Tsugaru Plain. Additionally, the area extending from the Seihoku-Go Area to the [[:en:Tsugaru Peninsula|Tsugaru Peninsula]] is sometimes called "Oku-Tsugaru" (奥津軽). Other geographical distinctions include the following: - The [[:en:Natsudomari Peninsula|Natsudomari Peninsula]] ([[:en:Hiranai|Hiranai Town]] area) was part of {{ill|Nukanobu District|ja|糠部郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nukanobu District|qq=|1=Nukanobu district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11604784}} until the early [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. - The coastal area around [[:en:Mutsu Bay|Mutsu Bay]] was referred to as {{ill|Sotogahama|ja|外ヶ浜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sotogahama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 外が浜|1=Sotogahama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11252073}} and was not historically considered part of Tsugaru until the early modern period. For more details, refer to the subdivisions and regional classifications under the [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] administrative system and the [[:en:Meteorological Agency|Meteorological Agency]] regional divisions. == History == Tsugaru appears in historical records as early as 655 CE in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where it is described as part of the distant territories inhabited by the [[:en:Emishi|Emishi]]. The region’s name has been recorded in various forms, including "Tsukaru," "Higashitsu," and "Tsukari." During the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], it was governed by the {{ill|Andō clan|ja|安東氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Andō clan|qq=|1=Andō clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11450838}}, before being taken over by the [[:en:Nanbu clan|Nanbu clan]] in the 14th century. The region became the domain of the [[:en:Tsugaru clan|Tsugaru clan]] during the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}. In the modern era, following the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], Tsugaru became part of Aomori Prefecture. == Climate == The Tsugaru region has a {{ill|Sea of Japan climate|ja|日本海側気候|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea of Japan climate|qq=|1=Japan Sea side climate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11086452}}, characterized by heavy snowfall in winter, especially in the mountainous areas. Summers are relatively short, with occasional high temperatures reaching 30°C due to the [[:en:Foehn wind|Föhn phenomenon]] in the southern areas. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|ja|南部地方 (青森県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanbu region (Aomori Prefecture)|qq=|1=Nanbu region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11408519}} * {{ill|Shimokita region|ja|下北地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokita region|qq=|1=Shimokita region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360647}} * {{ill|Seihoku-Go Area|ja|西北五地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seihoku-Go Area|qq=|1=Seihoku-Go Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11627298}} * [[:en:Tsugaru District, Mutsu|Tsugaru District (Mutsu Province)]] * [[:en:Aomori Prefecture|Aomori Prefecture]] * [[:en:Tsugaru dialect|Tsugaru dialect]] * [[:en:Tsugaru-jamisen|Tsugaru shamisen]] * {{ill|Tsugaru and Nanbu|ja|津軽と南部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru and Nanbu|qq=|1=Tsugaru and Nanbu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556385}} * {{ill|Tsugaru elections|ja|津軽選挙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru elections|qq=|1=Tsugaru elections|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215206}} == External Links == * [http://www.net.pref.aomori.jp/tugarukoiki/ Tsugaru Wide-Area Alliance] (Chūkō Nankoku Area municipal federation) {{translated page|ja|津軽地方|version=104351515|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsugaru region]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsugaru region]] [[sn:E6%B4%A5%E8%BB%BD%E5%9C%B0%E6%96%B9]] [[ja:津軽地方]] [[zh:津輕地方]]
